{
  "claims": [
    "Students who suffer from mental or physical illnesses that require them to miss substantial amounts of school should be afforded a pathway to graduate from high school on time.",
    "The number of adolescents reporting poor mental health is increasing.",
    "In 2021, more than 42% of students felt persistently sad or hopeless, and nearly 30% of students experienced poor mental health.",
    "In 2021, more than 22% of students seriously considered attempting suicide, and 10% of students did attempt suicide.",
    "Students with poor mental health may struggle with further health and behavioral risks, including increased risk of drug use or violence.",
    "Students with chronic health conditions may miss school more often than others, which may result in an impact on academic performance.",
    "Students suffering from mental or physical illnesses that result in frequent absence from school should not be academically penalized for the status of their mental or physical health."
  ]
}
